content: src/index.ts must contain a top-level `const VERSION = "x.y.z";` literal — the publish workflow (.github/workflows/publish.yml) grep-extracts this line and aborts publish if it disagrees with package.json. Do not refactor VERSION into a function call, computed expression, or different file without updating the workflow regex (`grep -oP 'const VERSION = "\K[^"]+' src/index.ts`).

Package name is `@os-eco/warren-cli` (not `@os-eco/warren` as suggested in SPEC §1, which marks it TBD). The publish workflow npm-views `@os-eco/warren-cli` to gate publishing — keep this name in package.json or the workflow short-circuits. Sibling tools follow the same `*-cli` pattern (`@os-eco/seeds-cli`, `@os-eco/burrow-cli`).

src/ subdirectory tree (core, registry, projects, runs, burrow-client, supervisor, server, db, cli, ui) is laid out by SPEC §7 but materialised lazily — each phase (warren-35db onward) creates the files it needs. Phase 0 only ships src/index.ts (VERSION) + src/cli/main.ts (commander wiring) + src/index.test.ts (smoke test). Do not pre-stub empty modules; let later phases own their files.

name: Quality gates triplet
description: Quality gates are `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` (the same triplet CONTRIBUTING.md and .github/workflows/ci.yml require). Biome config is tab indent + 100-col, with `noUnusedVariables/Imports/FunctionParameters` and `noExplicitAny` as errors — same posture as @os-eco/seeds. Run all three before committing; CI will reject on any failure.

name: Sibling repo configs
description: When scaffolding new pieces (db config, server skeleton, CLI command shape), reference `/Users/jayminwest/Projects/os-eco/burrow` and `/Users/jayminwest/Projects/os-eco/seeds` first — they pin the os-eco-wide conventions warren follows: tsconfig (strict + noUncheckedIndexedAccess + allowImportingTsExtensions), biome (tab + 100-col), commander CLI shape, drizzle.config.ts pattern. Burrow is the closest in domain (HTTP server + drizzle + supervisor) and the most useful template.

title: Wire @os-eco/burrow-cli via file:../burrow until burrow is published
rationale: Burrow's HttpClient (lib/http-client.ts) is warren's only seam to the runtime, but @os-eco/burrow-cli isn't on npm yet. file:../burrow makes the sibling repo install resolvable in this monorepo layout (bun install creates a symlink-ish copy under node_modules/@os-eco/burrow-cli). Flip to a semver range when burrow ships — warren-345f tracks. Don't inline-copy burrow's wire types; they need to stay in lockstep with burrow's server responses.

description: TypeScript test stubs for typeof fetch fail strict typecheck because fetch's signature requires a preconnect() method that bare async functions lack (Property 'preconnect' is missing in type ...). Resolution: cast via a small helper, e.g. function stub(impl): typeof fetch { return impl as unknown as typeof fetch; }. Don't try to satisfy the full Function-with-preconnect shape — preconnect is never invoked by fetch consumers in tests.
resolution: Use a stub() helper in test files that does 'return impl as unknown as typeof fetch'. Lint allows it; tsc passes; runtime is unaffected because consumer code never calls fetch.preconnect.

description: Bun's biome formatter rewrites multi-line throw-with-options-object into a single line up to lineWidth=100, but only after the lint rule fires. Long error messages with template literals + recoveryHint object commonly fail format check on first write.
resolution: Run 'bun run lint:fix' before committing. The auto-fix is mechanical and safe; the rewrite is purely cosmetic.

content: UI build entrypoint from repo root: 'bun run build:ui' shells 'cd src/ui && bun install && bun run build'. Output lands at src/ui/dist/{index.html, assets/*}. Server picks it up via WARREN_UI_DIST_DIR (defaults to <cwd>/src/ui/dist via src/server/config.ts:resolveUiDistDir). UI handler (src/server/ui.ts) refuses /agents, /projects, /runs, /healthz, /readyz prefixes and falls through to index.html for any other GET so the SPA can render deep links.

Container ENTRYPOINT is 'bun run src/supervisor/main.ts', NOT warren or burrow directly. The supervisor (src/supervisor/main.ts) owns boot ordering (spawn burrow, wait for socket, then spawn warren), signal forwarding (SIGTERM/SIGINT to both children with 5s grace), and burrow restart policy (5-in-60s budget). Both Dockerfile and fly.toml reference the supervisor as the entrypoint.

title: Two-stage Dockerfile: ui-builder + runtime-from-burrow-base
rationale: Stage 1 (oven/bun:1.1 AS ui-builder) builds the React/Vite SPA and emits dist/. Stage 2 extends ghcr.io/jayminwest/burrow-base:0.2.0 (which provides bun + bwrap + uidmap + burrow CLI), installs the four os-eco CLIs warren shells out to (canopy/seeds/mulch/sapling), copies warren source, then COPYs the prebuilt UI bundle from stage 1. This avoids shipping node_modules for the UI in the final image and avoids running 'bun run build:ui' inside the runtime stage where the Vite toolchain isn't otherwise needed.

content: docker-compose.yml applies the four bwrap-friendly security flags (apparmor=unconfined, seccomp=unconfined, systempaths=unconfined, cap_add: SYS_ADMIN) to the warren service. fly.toml does NOT set these — Fly Machines are Firecracker VMs, not containers, and bwrap nests on a stock Machine kernel. Same image, different container posture per orchestrator. See burrow/DEPLOY.md for the underlying recipe (mx-94901b/mx-c085ba).

description: Biome's lint/style/noNonNullAssertion auto-fix (lint:fix) replaces 'arr[idx]!' with 'arr[idx]?', silently turning a compile-time guarantee into a runtime no-op. With noUncheckedIndexedAccess in tsconfig, indexed access is T|undefined; '!' was the assertion the array slot is populated, '?' makes the call (resolveExit, etc.) silently no-op when the slot is empty — masking test bugs.
resolution: Refactor the test to a for-loop that explicitly throws on undefined: 'for (let i...) { const child = arr[i]; if (child === undefined) throw new Error(...); child.method(); }'. Don't accept the auto-fix.

title: Warren is not published to npm; container image is the only distribution channel
rationale: Warren's CLI surface (serve, register-agent, add-project, run, doctor) is server-internal — every command operates on warren's local sqlite and the burrow unix socket inside the deployed container, not on an operator's laptop. Cross-checked the os-eco tree: nothing imports @os-eco/warren-cli or shells out to 'warren'/'wr' (unlike burrow, which overstory/sapling/the warren Dockerfile itself all install globally). The Dockerfile also bakes warren via COPY of source rather than 'bun install -g @os-eco/warren-cli', so even warren's own deploy did not consume itself from npm. The container image (Fly Machine / docker-compose, per SPEC §10.2/10.3) is the canonical artifact. Maintaining the publish flow (NPM_TOKEN secret, version-sync check against src/index.ts, CHANGELOG discipline) was overhead with zero consumers, and a published @os-eco/warren-cli would have invited users to 'npm i -g' it and hit confusing failures running 'wr serve' outside the container.

description: When warren's supervisor calls Bun.spawn({ cmd: ['burrow', ...], cwd: '/app' }), Bun resolves 'burrow' via /app/node_modules/.bin/burrow BEFORE walking PATH. So a Dockerfile that 'bun install -g' a newer @os-eco/burrow-cli to /usr/local/bin/burrow has zero effect on the supervisor's spawned process — it runs the lockfile-pinned local copy. Bumping a CLI that warren both depends on (library) AND ships globally requires updating BOTH the Dockerfile pin AND package.json + bun.lock; otherwise the supervisor runs the stale local bin.
resolution: Bump @os-eco/burrow-cli (and any other dual-purpose CLI) in three places: Dockerfile global install line, package.json deps, and bun.lock (regenerate via 'bun install'). Verify post-rebuild: docker compose exec warren sh -c 'for pid in /proc/[0-9]*; do tr "\\0" " " < "$pid/cmdline"; done | grep burrow.serve' — if the path is /app/node_modules/.bin/burrow, the lockfile pin is what's running.

name: claude-code-postinstall-not-run-by-bun-install-g
description: @anthropic-ai/claude-code declares a postinstall script that downloads the platform-native 'claude' binary. Bun's 'bun install -g' skips lifecycle scripts by default for safety, so the bin shim at /usr/local/bin/claude just prints 'Error: claude native binary not installed' and exits 1. Workaround in Dockerfile: after 'bun install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@<v>', run 'bun run /root/.bun/install/global/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code/install.cjs' to invoke the postinstall manually. Same pattern likely applies to any npm package that uses postinstall to fetch native binaries.

biome's noFallthroughSwitchClause flags a switch case whose body ends in process.exit() — biome only treats break/return/throw/continue as terminators, not Node's never-returning calls. CI lint failed on scripts/acceptance/run.ts where 'case "-h": printHelp(); process.exit(0);' fell through to default.
resolution: Add an explicit 'break;' after process.exit(0) (it's unreachable but satisfies the rule). Same pattern applies to any switch-case body terminating in process.exit / never-returning helpers.

content: release workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml) tags + creates GH release on every push to main where package.json version doesn't yet exist as v$VERSION on origin tags. To cut a new release: bump package.json + src/index.ts VERSION (must match — workflow verifies), add a CHANGELOG.md '## [X.Y.Z]' section, push to main. No npm publish (warren ships as container per mx-834a3a). v0.1.0 was tagged manually before the workflow landed.

description: bun-types' Serve.Options is XOR(HostnamePortServeOptions, UnixServeOptions); idleTimeout is declared only on the TCP variant, so Bun.serve({ unix, fetch, idleTimeout }) fails typecheck with 'Type number is not assignable to type undefined'. Bun's runtime DOES honor idleTimeout on unix sockets — the type is the gap.
resolution: Cast through unknown when binding: const opts = { unix, fetch, idleTimeout } as unknown as Parameters<typeof Bun.serve>[0]; return Bun.serve(opts);

content: Dockerfile must symlink /app/src/cli/main.ts → /usr/local/bin/warren AND /usr/local/bin/wr after the COPY . /app step. The five os-eco CLIs are installed via 'bun install -g' but warren itself is not, so its package.json bin entries (warren, wr) only land on PATH via an explicit symlink. main.ts is +x with a '#!/usr/bin/env bun' shebang so the symlink runs as-is. Without it 'docker compose exec warren warren <subcmd>' fails with 'executable file not found in PATH' and operators must fall back to 'bun /app/src/cli/main.ts'.

Runtime image installs curl alongside bubblewrap/uidmap/git/ca-certificates so first-run diagnostics against the burrow unix socket (curl --unix-socket /var/run/burrow.sock http://localhost/healthz) work out of the box — no bun -e fetch() workarounds. ~1MB image cost; debugging time saved is hours. Don't drop curl when slimming the image without first wiring an alternative diagnostic path.

description: drizzle-kit generate (v0.31.10) for SQLite emits CREATE INDEX with the descending expression wrapped in backticks: CREATE INDEX foo ON runs (`project_id`,`"started_at" DESC`). SQLite then treats "started_at" DESC as a single literal column name and migrate fails at runtime with 'no such column: "started_at" DESC'. The original 0000_init.sql (older drizzle-kit) used the correct form (project_id,"started_at" DESC) — no backticks around the expression.
resolution: After 'bunx drizzle-kit generate', hand-edit the new migration .sql to drop the backticks around any indexed expression like `"col" DESC` → "col" DESC. The bun-sqlite migrator runs migrations on every openDatabase call, so a bad index expression surfaces immediately in any unit test that opens an in-memory db.

description: Warren's Dockerfile global bun install lands in /root/.bun/install/global by default, but burrow's sandbox SYSTEM_RO_MOUNTS only ro-binds /usr, /etc, /lib, /lib64, /bin, /sbin, /opt — so /usr/local/bin/{sd,ml,cn,sapling,burrow} symlinks dangle inside the sandbox and the agent can't run any os-eco CLI.
resolution: Set ENV BUN_INSTALL=/usr/local before 'bun install -g'. Bun then stores packages under /usr/local/install/global/node_modules/, which sits under the /usr ro-bind, so symlinks resolve. Update the explicit claude-code postinstall path to match (/usr/local/install/global/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code/install.cjs).

title: Acceptance harness wraps burrow serve with a custom entry that registers stub-shell + uses no-bwrap spawn
rationale: Two upstream gaps blocked acceptance scenarios that need a real running agent: (1) burrow serve's AgentRegistry only carries built-ins; declarative [[agents]] in burrow.toml are read for toolchain mounts but never registered into the runtime registry (node_modules/@os-eco/burrow-cli/src/runtime/registry.ts comment is aspirational). (2) burrow's runSandboxed needs CAP_SYS_ADMIN to mount /proc in a fresh userns; ordinary dev/CI containers without the bwrap-friendly compose flags fail with 'Operation not permitted'. The wrapper at scripts/acceptance/lib/burrow-with-stub.ts imports Client.open() and runServeCommand from @os-eco/burrow-cli, programmatically registers the stub-shell AgentConfig via loadAgentConfig + client.agents.register, and passes a no-bwrap SpawnFn (direct Bun.spawn against the workspace dir) through dispatcherOptions.spawn. inproc.ts's spawnBurrow then launches 'bun run burrow-with-stub.ts' instead of 'burrow serve'. Production warren still runs 'burrow serve' via the supervisor — the wrapper is acceptance-only.

content: Acceptance container mode (scripts/acceptance/lib/compose.ts) does NOT bind-mount the host canopy/sample fixtures into the container — the production deploy is fixture-free, so the harness mirrors that shape. Scenarios that need fixtures or that drive process control (kill/restart) declare modes:['in-proc']; container mode runs only scenarios 01 (boot/healthz/readyz) and 13 (container smoke). Override yaml in tmp inlines WARREN_API_TOKEN, WARREN_BURROW_NO_AUTH=1, blank CANOPY_REPO_URL; never reads .env. Each run uses a unique compose project name (warren-acceptance-<random>) so parallel runs don't collide, and a random ephemeral host port mapped to :8080. Boot timeout is 120s (first run builds the image); teardown is 'docker compose down -v --timeout 5'.

package.json exposes 'acceptance' (in-proc default) and 'acceptance:container' (compose-based). ACCEPTANCE.md at repo root is the operator runbook for V1 — covers the trinity (bun test/lint/typecheck), in-proc + container modes, and the three manual gates that the automated harness deliberately doesn't try to cover (--real claude-code run, fly deploy, UI smoke). Manual gates exist because token cost / cloud time / visual inspection can't be cheaply automated; SPEC §11.E–§11.G post-mortems are the canonical list of failure shapes the manual gates catch (warren-67cc, warren-a69a, warren-1eaa, warren-1a09, branchPushed-requires-both-reap-and-sandbox-git).

title: warren-config formats: triggers.yaml + defaults.json (per pl-5d74 step 1)
rationale: ROADMAP R-02 locks YAML for triggers (cron expressions read better than TOML/JSON, arrays-of-objects are clean) and the plan keeps defaults as JSON for symmetry with the rest of os-eco's UI/API JSON surface and to avoid forcing a YAML round-trip on the simpler defaults file. Note: ROADMAP R-02 originally proposed defaults.yaml; pl-5d74 deviates to defaults.json — captured here so future readers see one source of truth before the ROADMAP edit lands in step 8 (warren-8107).

title: YAML parser: js-yaml ^4.1.1 (matches mulch + overstory)
rationale: pl-5d74 risk #2 calls for using the same YAML lib as siblings to avoid ecosystem drift. mulch (^4.1.1 in deps + ^4.0.9 @types/js-yaml in devDeps) and overstory (@types/js-yaml ^4.0.9 in devDeps) both standardize on js-yaml; seeds/canopy/sapling/greenhouse/burrow have no YAML dep. Picked js-yaml@^4.1.1 + @types/js-yaml@^4.0.9 to mirror mulch's pin exactly. Considered the 'yaml' package — equally maintained but would introduce a second YAML lib into the os-eco install graph.

title: Cron lib: croner ^10.0.1 for R-06 scheduler
rationale: pl-2f15 step 1 picks the cron parser for the R-06 scheduler. Plan approach evaluated four alternatives and selected croner because: (1) small (~30 KB), no native deps, no postinstall scripts — fits warren's tiny-dep posture; (2) tz-aware with explicit DST handling, which a homegrown 5-token parser would skip and later regret (plan risk #2); (3) actively maintained, MIT-licensed, single file; (4) supports both 5-token (Vixie) and 6-token (seconds) grammars, which lets warren-config keep its loose 'cron expression validation' (mx-40fe51) and defer strict parsing to fire time. Bring-our-own parser was rejected — cron grammar corner cases (step values, ranges, DOW vs DOM, 6-token seconds) would either be wrong or grow into croner anyway.

content: Adding a drizzle migration: edit schema.ts → bun run db:generate emits src/db/migrations/NNNN_<random-codename>.sql + meta/NNNN_snapshot.json + appends to _journal.json. Then (1) rename the SQL to a descriptive NNNN_<verb_noun>.sql, (2) update the matching 'tag' in meta/_journal.json so the migrator can find it, (3) run 'bunx biome format --write' on the new snapshot + journal — drizzle-kit emits 2-space indent but biome.json enforces tabs.

When R-06 (cron scheduler) ships new trigger kinds or new TriggerSchema fields, update three places in lockstep: ROADMAP R-06 entry, SPEC §11.I (and §11.H if the warren-config schema changes), and acceptance scenario 15 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/15-triggers-roundtrip.ts). New TriggerSchema fields must stay additive (all-optional) so existing triggers.yaml files keep parsing — warren-config (R-02) and the dispatcher (R-06) co-own the schema. Sibling lockstep convention to mx-5339d5 (R-02).

User-facing positioning (README, SPEC §1/§2, package.json description, CLAUDE.md opener, Dockerfile/fly.toml comments) leads with what warren *does* — spawn sandboxed agents at GitHub repos, watch live, steer, get a branch — NOT with the four-tool composition. The os-eco data-plane tools (canopy/mulch/seeds/sapling) are 'opt-in built-in features' that ship in every image but are not required for a fresh install: claude-code is inline, canopy activates on CANOPY_REPO_URL, mulch/seeds activate on per-project .mulch/.seeds/ directories, sapling is just another inline agent. When updating docs that name-drop the four tools as prerequisites, demote them to a 'Power features' section (warren-e576).

.env.example documents multi-provider keys (OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, PI_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY, MISTRAL_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY) commented out by default (warren-f8c0). The supervisor inherits container env into both warren and burrow children via Bun.spawn without an explicit env: {} — no docker-compose.yml change needed. Burrow's piRuntime.envPassthrough is what actually forwards the keys into the sandbox; warren just makes sure they reach the burrow process.

Drizzle-generated migrations land with a random name (e.g. 0006_dusty_jimmy_woo.sql). Rename to the project convention (0006_add_run_cost_columns.sql) and update the matching tag in src/db/migrations/meta/_journal.json in the same change — drizzle reads the tag from the journal, not the filename. The companion meta/NNNN_snapshot.json stays as-is (keyed by idx, not name).

'bun run lint' (biome check --error-on-warnings .) descends into .claude/worktrees/<branch>/ and fails with 'Found a nested root configuration' when agent worktrees exist locally — each worktree has its own biome.json. Workaround: run 'bunx biome check --error-on-warnings src/ scripts/ drizzle.config.ts' to scan explicit paths and bypass the nested-root detection. Pre-existing local-environment condition; biome.json on main is unchanged.

Bumping @os-eco/burrow-cli to pick up a new built-in runtime (e.g. piRuntime in 0.2.8) is a pin-only change in three lockstep locations (package.json + bun.lock + Dockerfile; per CLAUDE.md 'Relationship to burrow'). No warren source changes needed — burrow's BUILT_IN_RUNTIMES is loaded by the supervisor's burrow serve. The acceptance harness (scripts/acceptance/lib/burrow-with-stub.ts) keeps its declarative stub registration for the new agent because the agent binary (e.g. pi) isn't installed in CI/dev; production warren talks to burrow's real runtime. Quality gates after bump: bun install → bun test + tsc + biome.

description: Bun-based docker images (oven/bun:1.2) provide a node-compat shim at /usr/local/bun-node-fallback-bin/node, but that directory is NOT on the PATH burrow exposes inside bwrap (only /usr/local/bin is bind-mounted). CLIs with #!/usr/bin/env node shebangs (like @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent) fail immediately inside the sandbox with 'env: node: No such file or directory'.
resolution: Add a Dockerfile symlink before WORKDIR: ln -s /usr/local/bun-node-fallback-bin/node /usr/local/bin/node. Confirmed working for pi v0.74.0; bun's shim handles its node-script semantics fine. Same approach generalizes to any node-shebanged CLI installed via 'bun install -g'.

content: Warren version bump for the R-07 disk-seam closure landed as 0.2.0 (warren plan pl-a31c, parent warren-0a83) — the minor bump signals that warren↔burrow is now HTTP-only on every code path except reap's mulch_merge + branch_push. package.json + src/index.ts must move in lockstep (release.yml grep-checks); CHANGELOG.md 0.2.0 entry references burrow plan bur-pl-2467 as the burrow-side R-07 origin.

WARREN_RUN_BRANCH_PREFIX (warren-9993) env loader lives in src/runs/branch.ts loadRunBranchPrefixFromEnv — same lenient shape as loadAutoOpenPrConfigFromEnv (src/runs/pr.ts): unset/whitespace/invalid → undefined, never throws. Wired in three places: src/server/main.ts bootServer reads once and threads via ServerDeps.runBranchPrefixDefault and BootSchedulerInput.runBranchPrefixDefault; src/cli/commands/run.ts defaults deps.runBranchPrefixDefault to a fresh loadRunBranchPrefixFromEnv() call (passing null forces the built-in 'burrow' for tests). Documented in .env.example below the WARREN_AUTO_OPEN_PR block.

name: acceptance pi declarative outputFormat gap
description: The acceptance harness registers pi as a declarative agent (scripts/acceptance/lib/burrow-with-stub.ts PI_AGENT_CONFIG with outputFormat:'raw-text'). Burrow's declarative agent only supports raw-text / stream-json / jsonl-claude (burrow src/runtime/declarative.ts:130-134) — there is no jsonl-pi outputFormat. So even after warren-17a4's in-stream cost extraction landed, scenario 16 (16-pi-parity-smoke.ts) still can't assert non-null cost_usd / tokens_* — burrow wraps each pi-shaped JSONL line as {kind:'text', stream:'stdout'} and the bridge's turn_end predicate never matches. Three unblock paths: (1) add jsonl-pi as a declarative outputFormat upstream, (2) install pi binary in CI + switch harness to real piRuntime, (3) register a built-in-style wrapper using parsePiEvents directly.

content: Drizzle migration meta files (src/db/migrations/meta/NNNN_snapshot.json, _journal.json) emitted by drizzle-kit generate are indented with two spaces by default, but biome.json formats them with tabs. After 'bun run db:generate', run 'bun run biome format --write src/db/migrations/meta/NNNN_snapshot.json src/db/migrations/meta/_journal.json' or 'bun run lint' will fail. Also format the generated 0008_snapshot.json the same way. (warren-135b)

name: Bun.TOML.parse is built-in
description: Bun ships native TOML parsing as Bun.TOML.parse(string) — throws on malformed input, returns plain JS object. No external TOML dependency needed in warren (used by src/server-config/load.ts for warren.toml). Empty/whitespace/comments-only input returns {}. Confirmed against bun 1.3.6.

content: warren's named volume 'warren_data' (declared in docker-compose.yml) lands on the host as 'warren_warren_data' because docker compose prefixes named volumes with the project name. When running one-shot containers against the data volume (sqlite surgery, manual db inspection), mount warren_warren_data. Verify with 'docker inspect warren --format {{json .Mounts}}' first. Mounting the unprefixed name silently creates a fresh empty volume — symptom is /data appearing empty when warren clearly populated it.

description: node_modules/@os-eco/burrow-cli stale per-file symlinks: warren's install of @os-eco/burrow-cli pinned at ^0.3.0 was a local-link install (each src file symlinked to ../burrow/src/...). When burrow added new files post-install (e.g. src/runtime/parsers/pi.ts after warren-17a4 / burrow-d949 landed), the symlinks didn't auto-populate. Symptom: tsc --noEmit fails with 'Cannot find module @os-eco/burrow-cli/src/runtime/parsers/pi.ts or its corresponding type declarations' even though the source file exists in the linked burrow checkout; bun test also fails to resolve the import.
resolution: rm -rf node_modules/@os-eco/burrow-cli && bun install — force-refreshes the install with the current registry tarball (which includes pi.ts because 459f7a2 predates burrow's 0.3.0 release commit 0639971). A plain 'bun install' is a no-op when the lockfile is satisfied; the rm is required to defeat the cache.

content: When R-13 (Postgres backend) ships schema or env-contract changes, update docs in lockstep across SEVEN surfaces: SPEC.md §3.2 (V1 non-goals list), §6 (Tech Stack DB row + 'No Postgres' line), §10.1/§10.2 (Deploy), §11.J (org-readiness direction); README.md (V1 today + 'Where this is going' list + Fly.io deploy snippet); ROADMAP.md (R-13 entry + Wave-2 sequencing list); .env.example (storage section); fly.toml (operator-workflow comment); docker-compose.yml (volume comment). Pattern mirrors mx-5339d5 (R-02) and mx-fae553 (R-06). The shipped doc edit landed via seed warren-bca1 (pl-f17e step 9, 2026-05-14).

description: R-13 Postgres support shipped half-complete in v0.3.2. CHANGELOG announced 'warren now supports Postgres as an opt-in backend via WARREN_DB_URL=postgres://...' but the repo layer (src/db/repos/*.ts) uses sqlite-only drizzle APIs internally: .get(), .run(), .all(), sync db.transaction(). Outer wiring (URL parsing, openDatabase, migrations, schema parity) is real and works; the moment any repo method runs against pg, it crashes. Two dialect guards (src/server/main.ts:createReposForDialect, src/cli/context.ts:withCliDb) backstop with a fail-fast pointing at pl-f17e step 7 marker. Lesson: when a feature ships via multi-step plan, the CHANGELOG should describe what's actually wired end-to-end, not what the plan title claims.
resolution: Tracked under plan pl-f1be (warren-2680). Approach: add dialect-polymorphic drizzle adapter (pickOne/pickAll/runWrite/runInTransaction), migrate all 7 repos to it smallest-first, widen createRepos to AnyWarrenDb, delete guards, add CI postgres matrix, validate via scenario 19, then retry dogfood deploy. Until shipped, stick with sqlite-on-volume and warn against WARREN_DB_URL=postgres:// in operator docs.

content: When connecting node-postgres to Supabase, append 'uselibpqcompat=true&sslmode=require' to the connection string. Without it, pg-connection-string v2 treats sslmode=require as verify-full, and Node's default trust store rejects Supabase's pooler cert chain with SELF_SIGNED_CERT_IN_CHAIN. The uselibpqcompat flag is the documented escape hatch (warning emitted by deprecatedSslModeWarning at pg-connection-string/index.js:216). Companion: when sourcing .env files that contain pg URLs with '&' in the query string, single-quote the value so 'set -a; source <file>' doesn't fork on the ampersand. fly secrets import strips surrounding quotes per dotenv conventions, so the URL Fly stores is clean. Both gotchas discovered 2026-05-14 during dogfood deploy — re-validate when warren-2680 ships.

.github/workflows/ci.yml runs two parallel jobs: 'ci' (lint + typecheck + sqlite 'bun test') and 'ci-postgres' (a second 'bun test' against a postgres:16 services container with job-level env WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=postgres + WARREN_TEST_PG_URL=postgres://warren:warren@127.0.0.1:5432/warren_test). The pg job mirrors the sqlite job's test step only — lint + typecheck are dialect-independent and live in 'ci' alone. Repo test suites (src/db/repos/*.test.ts) iterate both dialects via 'if (isPostgresTestEnabled()) suite("postgres")', so 'bun test' under the pg env naturally adds the pg suites on top of the sqlite ones. When adding a new repo or schema, mirror the same suite() pattern so it picks up pg coverage in CI for free.

When a CHANGELOG entry overstates a release (e.g. R-13 in v0.3.2 announcing Postgres support that crash-looped on first repo call), amend the original release section with a brief blockquote 'Correction (date):' callout that points at the follow-up plan, then add the actual completion as a new [Unreleased] entry. The blockquote preserves the audit trail without rewriting history; the [Unreleased] entry is where the real fix is recorded.

description: Fly secrets carried over from local docker-compose can poison the Fly deploy. Specifically: CANOPY_REPO_URL=file:///canopy-source (the bind-mount path docker-compose injects via the CANOPY_SOURCE_DIR volume) was set on the warren-deployed Fly app from an earlier session and surfaced after the 2026-05-14 dogfood deploy as 'canopy_unavailable: /canopy-source does not appear to be a git repository' on POST /agents/refresh and ok:false on /readyz canopy_clone + canopy_clean. The file:/// scheme only resolves inside the compose bind-mount; Fly Machines have no /canopy-source path. Built-in agents (claude-code, sapling, pi) ship inline and do not need canopy, so canopy is opt-in per SPEC §1. README's fly.toml comment block correctly has CANOPY_REPO_URL commented out as the default.
resolution: Run 'fly secrets unset CANOPY_REPO_URL -a <app>' on any Fly deploy that doesn't have a real https git URL for a canopy library. The unset bounces the machine; /readyz then reports 'no canopy library configured (using built-in agents only)' with ok:true. Audit fly secrets list after first deploy and remove any local-dev-only values.

name: fly-supabase-dogfood-end-to-end-shape
description: Successful warren Fly+Supabase dogfood, 2026-05-14, after pl-f1be (R-13 completion). Observable shape: (1) fly secrets set WARREN_DB_URL='postgresql://postgres:<pw>@db.<ref>.supabase.co:5432/postgres?sslmode=require&uselibpqcompat=true' (single-quote the value when sourcing .env so '&' doesn't fork the shell; fly strips the quotes per dotenv conventions). (2) fly deploy + fly machine start. (3) GET /healthz returns 200. (4) GET /readyz with Bearer WARREN_API_TOKEN returns ok:true across all 11 checks: db_reachable.dialect=postgres, burrow_reachable, agents, canopy_clone (no canopy library configured), bwrap=bubblewrap 0.8.0, warren_config (no projects), preview_* gated on dialect=sqlite. (5) POST /projects gitUrl=<repo> → 201 with prj_<id>. (6) POST /runs agent=claude-code project=<id> prompt='print foo and exit' → 200 with run_<id>, state:queued. (7) Within ~4s, run transitions queued→running→succeeded; reap.completed event has branchPushed:true, commitsAhead:0 (no-op pushes are valid 'success' shape — the reap.empty_push warning event surfaces the work-loss case operators must watch for, per warren-f3bb / SPEC §11.G). pg paths exercised: ProjectsRepo INSERT/SELECT, RunsRepo INSERT/UPDATE/SELECT, EventsRepo INSERT (411 events), AgentsRepo SELECT, BurrowsRepo INSERT/SELECT. If POST /projects returns 'target path already exists' the previous (crashed) deploy stranded /data/projects/<owner>/<repo>; 'fly ssh console -C "rm -rf <path>"' clears it.

content: Dockerfile bakes pnpm@11.1.2 + npm@11.14.1 alongside the other JS CLIs via 'bun install -g' so per-run preview sidecars (R-19 / SPEC §11.L) can boot 'pnpm dev' / 'npm run dev' for projects that don't use bun (warren-810f). Both rely on the existing /usr/local/bin/node bun-shim symlink installed for pi compat (mx-27f385). When bumping these, treat it the same as the other CLI pins — version is a deliberate image-rebuild decision.

CI auto-deploy to Fly is wired as a deploy job in .github/workflows/release.yml that 'needs: release' and gates on needs.release.outputs.release == 'true', so no-op pushes to main don't redeploy. The release job exposes 'release' and 'version' via outputs:. Concurrency group 'fly-deploy-warren-deployed' serializes overlapping releases without cancelling. Token is provisioned via 'fly tokens create deploy -a warren-deployed --name github-actions --expiry 8760h | gh secret set FLY_API_TOKEN -R jayminwest/warren' — scoped to a single app, cannot list secrets or ssh. Uses superfly/flyctl-actions/setup-flyctl@master + 'flyctl deploy --remote-only --app warren-deployed'. Reference shape documented in README's 'Continuous deployment from GitHub Actions' section as the recipe operators can copy for their own warren installs.

name: self-validating-ci-release
description: When the CI release flow is the unit under test (e.g. adding a new deploy job), bundle the workflow change + a real code change into one version bump. The release proves out both: workflow correctness AND that the underlying code change ships to production. v0.3.5 shipped the deploy job AND the pg-init FK qualifier fix (a38c687) — pushing main → release.yml ran → tag created → deploy job fired → Fly machine rolled from v12→v13 → /readyz green with v0.3.4's preview probes (0/1001 ports, 0/20 live previews) now actually running in prod. A pure no-op version burn would have proved the workflow but had nothing to release; a workflow-only commit can't prove the deploy job fires without bumping the version. The bundle is the dogfood.

content: Biome lint/style/noNonNullAssertion is enforced in test files too. Patterns like 'expect(dec.decode(out!))' where out is T|null fail the lint gate even after expect(out).not.toBeNull(). Refactor with a helper that narrows + throws on null (e.g. function inject(html): string { const out = injectBaseHref(...); if (out === null) throw new Error('expected ...'); return dec.decode(out); }) so subsequent uses are typed-non-null.

Passing Uint8Array directly to 'new Response(body, init)' fails tsc strict-mode type-check on Bun: TS reports 'Uint8Array<ArrayBufferLike> is not assignable to BodyInit' even though the runtime accepts it. The mismatch is the parameterized Uint8Array<ArrayBufferLike> in current Bun lib types vs BodyInit's BufferSource union. Cast via 'startBytes as unknown as BodyInit' at the new Response call site. Same Uint8Array works as ReadableStreamController enqueue arg without a cast.

description: Per-run preview sidecar runs Node-targeted projects (next/vite/remix) on Bun, not real Node. Dockerfile:89 symlinks /usr/local/bin/node → /usr/local/bun-node-fallback-bin/node for pi shebang compat (warren-810f). warren-810f baked pnpm+npm for non-bun preview projects but reused the same shim, so 'pnpm dev' → next bin → exec node → loads under Bun. Bun has different built-in module coverage than Node; any dep requiring node:sqlite (and likely other modern node: built-ins) crashes immediately. Verified 2026-05-15 on warren-deployed run_jegavdzfbrpc against Next.js 14.2.24: previewFailureMessage was 'No such built-in module: node:sqlite / Bun v1.2.23'. Acceptance scenario 20-preview*.ts uses python3 -m http.server (mx-87d34c), so no Node runtime ever crosses the symlink — gap invisible in CI.
resolution: Install real Node alongside Bun in Dockerfile apt-get layer; relocate the bun-shim symlink off PATH and patch pi's shebang to #!/usr/bin/env bun (or symlink it where only pi resolves). Bumping oven/bun:1.2 to a newer tag (Bun 1.2.42+ adds node:sqlite) is whack-a-mole — fixes this error, leaves the architectural mismatch for the next missing built-in. Add a Node-runtime acceptance scenario (next or vite dev) so the gap can't reopen.

description: biome lint/suspicious/noExplicitAny treats 'as any' as a hard error even when preceded by a biome-ignore line comment; the suppression is ignored and lint fails. Tests that need to force a runtime type-mismatch must use 'as unknown as ParamsType' instead — e.g. 'as unknown as Parameters[typeof updateExtensions][3]' (using bracket-style notation here to dodge shell tokenization). Encountered while writing src/seeds-cli/extensions.test.ts rejection-path tests for updateExtensions.
resolution: Switched the offending casts to 'as unknown as Parameters[typeof updateExtensions][3]' (in source: angle-bracket generics). biome.json's noExplicitAny stays an error; tests work around it via unknown casts, not suppressions.

content: Adding a new opt-in bundled feature requires touching at least five surfaces in cross-cutting docs (CLAUDE.md feature list, README.md 'Power features' section + top-of-file pitch, SPEC.md §1 TL;DR + §2 framing + §11 dedicated subsection + §12 relationship table, CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] Added entry). Missing any of these surfaces the change asymmetrically (e.g. README mentions it but SPEC §12 doesn't). The opt-in framing must stay primary in §1 and the README pitch — fresh-install path remains standalone, integrations 'light up' when activated.
@os-eco/plot-cli version pinning

Bumping a co-tenanted CLI dep that gates a runtime enum (e.g. @os-eco/plot-cli's PLOT_EVENT_TYPES) needs a compile-time probe in addition to the package.json+bun.lock+Dockerfile triple-pin. Drop a one-line conditional-type assertion in the consuming facade (src/plot-client/types.ts) so typecheck fails fast if the dep regresses below the floor that exports the required enum member.

Phase-2 composition wiring docs ship as a §11.X.Y SPEC sub-subsection rather than a new top-level §11.Q. Smaller doc surface than mx-02ca3b's new-feature lockstep — only the diff that surfaces composition matters.

description: warren-0a6b (Bump @os-eco/burrow-cli double-pin) is gated on burrow-59cd shipping to npm. As of 2026-05-18, latest published @os-eco/burrow-cli is 0.3.1 (published 2026-05-14T17:33Z) which is what package.json (^0.3.1), bun.lock (0.3.1), and Dockerfile (@0.3.1) already pin. Verified node_modules/@os-eco/burrow-cli/src/server/handlers.ts createBurrow handler (line 404) still constructs BurrowUpInput without parsing body.env — so warren-e26f's PLOT_ID/PLOT_ACTOR are still silently dropped. No bump possible until burrow publishes 0.3.2+ with the body.env parse. Do not invent a placeholder version; re-check npm view @os-eco/burrow-cli@latest before reattempting.
resolution: Block on burrow-59cd. Re-run 'npm view @os-eco/burrow-cli@latest version' periodically; when a >0.3.1 version appears, verify it parses body.env in createBurrow, then bump all three pins (package.json + bun.lock via bun install + Dockerfile line 92), and proceed to warren-49d7 (flip scenario 25 soft-skip).

content: warren 0.4.1 patch-bumps the burrow-cli double-pin consumer-side update for burrow-59cd (body.env parsing on POST /burrows). CHANGELOG entry must name burrow-59cd as the unblocker and call out the scenario 25 flip from soft-skipped to hard-required for the env-reaches-sandbox / workspace plot append / plot.* mirror assertions. Pattern: when the warren patch closes a cross-repo gap (burrow fix → warren consumer bump), the CHANGELOG entry describes the gap, names the burrow seed id, and lists the three changed locations (double-pin, scenario flip, version bump in two places per mx-70e9e9).

name: burrow-cli-cascade-block-followup-bump
description: Cascade-block double-pin bump: when warren-side of a plan ships before the upstream burrow-cli release is on npm, the bump-step seeds correctly record cascade-block notes (warren-0a6b/warren-73ee, commits 9a12e6b/983d77a) and a follow-up seed (warren-c106) lands the deferred pin bump once 'npm view @os-eco/burrow-cli versions' lists the target version. Mechanical recipe: edit package.json + Dockerfile + run 'bun install' to update bun.lock (three locations per CLAUDE.md 'Relationship to burrow' convention), hand-bump warren package.json + src/index.ts VERSION, CHANGELOG entry citing the cascade gap, then 'bun run scripts/acceptance/run.ts --only 25' against in-proc to confirm env-reaches-sandbox hard-required assertions pass.

content: warren 0.4.7 patch release (warren-41f9, pl-5310 step 5) cuts the Plot UX shipment: plot_id format+existence validation (warren-bae5, step 2) + PlotDetail batch 'Dispatch all' button (warren-7c3f, step 3). Step 4 (warren-a4b7) ships as SPEC §11.Q design lock only — implementation gated on upstream seeds-cli warren-d519 and correctly scoped out per /release default 'always patch unless user requests minor/major'. Version bumped in package.json + src/index.ts in lockstep (mx-8f151f two-place-pin precedent). CHANGELOG.md gains [0.4.7] dated 2026-05-18 with Added/Changed/Docs subsections for the three commits since v0.4.6. README.md / CLAUDE.md untouched — no CLI-reference drift. Quality gates: lint + typecheck green; bun test shows the same 2 pre-existing BurrowClientPool real-burrow flakes already noted in warren-a4b7's commit (require real burrow, unrelated).

Bumping @os-eco/burrow-cli to pick up a new envPassthrough function-form (burrow 0.3.3, piRuntime.envPassthrough=piEnvPassthrough per burrow-6f3f) is a pin-only change in three lockstep locations (package.json + bun.lock + Dockerfile; per CLAUDE.md 'Relationship to burrow'). No warren source changes are required for the runtime contract itself — burrow's dispatcher does the work via applyRuntimeEnvPassthrough. The acceptance harness needs an opt-in lift to exercise the new shape: burrow-with-stub.ts overrides buildPiAcceptanceRuntime's envPassthrough with the real piEnvPassthrough from @os-eco/burrow-cli/src/runtime/pi.ts. Pattern is additive — static-array runtimes (claude-code, sapling, codex) compile unchanged.

Adding a new top-level API route requires updating THREE sync points: (1) src/server/handlers.ts ROUTE_TABLE entry, (2) src/server/handlers.ts API_PREFIXES top-level prefix list, (3) src/preview/proxy.ts WARREN_API_PATH_PREFIXES. The warren-63e1 invariant test (src/preview/proxy.test.ts:1203 'local WARREN_API_PATH_PREFIXES matches handlers.API_PREFIXES') asserts the latter two stay in sync. Missing #3 means preview-proxy referer-based routing won't recognize the new prefix. Discovered while adding POST /plot-plan-runs (warren-99b2 / SPEC §11.Q): bun test failed on the API-prefix invariant test until proxy.ts was bumped.

description: warren-fdd2's snapshot+restore wrapper for refreshProjectClone only wrapped 'git reset --hard origin/<ref>' — but the preceding 'git checkout --force <ref>' (refresh.ts:154) also wipes uncommitted modifications to tracked files. The host-side Plot appenders write to .plot/<id>.events.jsonl WITHOUT committing, so every host-appender write made before the LAST spawn cycle vanished: only the final child's run_dispatched + the auto-done status_changed survived on disk. plan_run_dispatched + earlier per-child run_dispatched were silently lost despite all unit tests in src/projects/refresh.test.ts passing (the test seam fakes the entire fn() so it never exercises the real git commands). Symptom only surfaces when an acceptance scenario reads .plot/<id>.events.jsonl after a multi-child plan-run.
resolution: Move the hadPlotPreReset probe + preservePlot wrapper to span BOTH 'git checkout --force <ref>' AND 'git reset --hard origin/<ref>' (src/projects/refresh.ts post-warren-af97). Snapshot now happens BEFORE any working-tree-touching command runs, so all uncommitted Plot appender writes get captured. Scenario 31 (warren-af97 / pl-f404 step 5) is the acceptance proof; scenario 27's plan_run_dispatched assertion also recovers.

name: warren-0.5.0-pl-0344-minor-bump
description: warren 0.5.0 minor release (warren-3395, pl-0344 step 17 / parent warren-769e, SPEC §11.O) ships the Plot-workbench loop end-to-end: db schema for interactive runs + paused state (warren-67b6), agent.pauseTimeoutMs config (warren-cd37), interactive run primitive (warren-1117), interactive HTTP API (warren-b3b9), blocking-question pause detector for batch runs (warren-2976), brainstorm + planner built-in agents (warren-3de8 / warren-543d), brainstorm dispatcher + formalize (warren-d22e), needs-attention API (warren-d693), UI Chat primitive (warren-ea98), PlotDetail interactive surfaces (warren-444c), paused-run surfacing (warren-4ea4), Plot-first home + sidebar badge (warren-f0e2), V1.5 click-to-merge + auto-sync (warren-8e39), V1.5 summary artifact view (warren-8917), and acceptance scenario 32 (warren-7cd9). Minor bump is correct per /release default override: 17-step plan delivering a primary UI shift (Plots as the home surface) + a new run mode + the paused run state + two new built-in agents is feature-set scale, not patch. Two-place pin (mx-70e9e9): package.json + src/index.ts both moved 0.4.8→0.5.0 in lockstep; release.yml grep verifies. CHANGELOG.md [0.5.0] dated 2026-05-23 enumerates every step with seed id and pl-0344 step number for traceability. Quality gates green: lint + typecheck clean, bun test 1925 pass / 1 fail (the documented BurrowClientPool real-burrow flake unrelated, per mx-9e62e2 precedent).

content: AGENTS.md at repo root (warren-0db9 / pl-7b06 step 2) is the agents.md-convention entrypoint for AI coding agents. It mirrors CLAUDE.md essentials (what warren is, burrow relationship, tech stack, build/test commands, quality gates trinity, TS conventions, two-place VERSION pin, .warren/config.yaml knobs, acceptance harness, session completion protocol, seeds+mulch onboarding) and declares CLAUDE.md authoritative on disagreement — so future cross-cutting doc updates touch CLAUDE.md first, then mirror AGENTS.md. Satisfies readiness criterion criterion:agents_md.

AGENTS.md drift guard (warren-9d2f / pl-7b06 step 3): scripts/validate-agents-md.ts is a pure-TS static checker invoked by 'bun run validate:agents-md' and a new CI step in .github/workflows/ci.yml (after typecheck, before bun test). It parses fenced bash blocks in AGENTS.md, asserts every 'bun run <X>' resolves to a script in package.json's scripts map, and asserts every backticked path-shaped token exists on disk. Heuristic skips: lines past '#' (shell comments), '@'-prefixed npm scopes, tokens ending in '...' (placeholders), bare extensions like '.ts', globs, URLs, and an explicit KNOWN_MISSING_PATHS allowlist for documented-but-not-checked-in paths (src/ui/dist build artifact, ../burrow/SPEC.md sibling repo, .warren/config.yaml per-project runtime file). Scoped to AGENTS.md only — CLAUDE.md has its own pre-existing drift (e.g. .warren/defaults.json refs) that should be cleaned up in a separate seed before adding it to TARGETS. Pure helpers are exported for unit testing (scripts/validate-agents-md.test.ts); main() is gated by import.meta.main so the test import doesn't process.exit.

CI runs 'bun run check:duplicates' (jscpd 4.2.4) via .jscpd.json (warren-61e9 / pl-7b06 step 11). Scan is restricted to src/**/*.{ts,tsx} and ignores tests, src/db/migrations/, src/db/schema/, __golden__, and src/ui/dist/ (auto-generated or structurally repetitive — they dominate raw signal at ~17.5% pre-filter). Filtered baseline is ~0.61% duplicated lines at minTokens=100; threshold:3 in .jscpd.json is the ratchet ceiling and only goes DOWN. Wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml after check:debt-markers. Top remaining clones live in plan-runs/coordinator.ts, server/main.ts route registration, runs/pr*.ts, and the warren↔ui api client mirror — fix those before tightening the threshold.

CI runs 'bun run check:deps' (knip 6.x in --dependencies mode) via knip.json (warren-d109 / pl-7b06 step 12). Covers root package and the src/ui workspace; CI runs 'bun run ui:install' before check:deps so knip can resolve src/ui imports. Findings are almost always real - fix with 'bun remove <dep>' (or 'cd src/ui && bun remove <dep>'); only ignore runtime/string-resolved peers (e.g. pino transports loaded by string name). Initial wiring removed chalk and pino-pretty from the root package.
Dependabot cooldown delays freshly published deps before PRs land.
prepare script sets core.hooksPath to scripts/hooks (relative, tracked directory) instead of copying hooks to .git/hooks/. Hooks fire on any checkout without needing bun install to have run first.

Test timing reporting (warren-cec7 / pl-7b06 step 16): CI runs 'bun run test:ci' which wraps 'bun test --reporter=junit --reporter-outfile=test-results/junit.xml'. 'bun run report:test-timing' (scripts/report-test-timing.ts) parses the XML, prints a markdown summary of slowest test files + top-20 slowest individual cases, and appends it to GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY when set. The JUnit XML is uploaded as the 'bun-test-junit' actions artifact (always(), so failed runs still publish timing). File-level totals are derived by summing per-case times because bun emits time=0 on the outer <testsuite> when a file has nested describe() blocks. test-results/ is gitignored.

Coverage guard (warren-e4b1 / pl-7b06 step 17): scripts/check-coverage.ts wraps 'bun test --coverage --coverage-reporter=text --coverage-reporter=lcov' and parses the 'All files' aggregate row from Bun's text reporter (lcov DA-line totals diverge from the text reporter — DA includes non-executable spans, so the lcov line% is ~42% while the text reporter says ~91%; always enforce against the text-reporter aggregate). Floors live in scripts/coverage-budgets.json (initial: functions 85.00, lines 90.00 against baseline 86.25 / 91.62 measured 2026-05-27) and the ratchet only goes UP — lowering them implies tests were deleted and needs a tracker reference. CI workflow runs 'check:coverage:ci' (adds --reporter=junit so report-test-timing still works) and uploads coverage/lcov.info as a build artifact alongside junit.xml. 'check:all' depends on the coverage ratchet so local quality gates and CI fail the same way. Bun's native [test] coverageThreshold in bunfig.toml does not gate process exit on Bun 1.2.23, so the wrapper script is required.

Code-quality metrics CI summary (warren-5b95 / pl-7b06 step 18): scripts/report-quality-metrics.ts emits a markdown table to GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY aggregating coverage % vs floors (prefers coverage/summary.json — now written by check-coverage.ts — for parity with Bun's text reporter; falls back to lcov.info), biome.json complexity-rule grandfather counts (cognitive + lines-per-function), file-size + debt-marker ratchet status, and bundle-size headroom. Wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml with if:always() after report:test-timing. Passive reporter — enforcement stays in the individual ratchet scripts.

X-Request-ID middleware (warren-30af / pl-7b06 step 19): src/server/request-id.ts owns the correlation-id seam. extractOrGenerateRequestId(request) honours an inbound X-Request-ID when it matches /^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]{1,128}$/ (narrow whitelist; rejects CRLF and log-spoof bytes) else mints crypto.randomUUID(). server.ts's fetchHandler wraps every dispatch: extracts/mints the id, builds a child logger via bindRequestIdLogger (uses pino .child when present else a manual shim injecting request_id), threads both into handleRequest, then calls stampRequestId on the returned Response so X-Request-ID is uniformly set on success/error/auth-deny/404/405/UI-fallback. RouteContext gained requestId:string so handlers can propagate downstream. Coverage in src/server/request-id.test.ts (unit) + src/server/request-id.wire.test.ts (e2e via startServer). Wire tests live in a sibling file because src/server/server.test.ts is at its frozen file-size budget — extend siblings rather than growing server.test.ts.

OpenAPI 3.1 schema guard (warren-b46b / pl-7b06 step 21): scripts/generate-openapi.ts reuses extractRoutes() from generate-docs.ts to derive docs/openapi.yaml from ROUTE_TABLE in src/server/handlers.ts. Path params (:id) become {id}, first path segment becomes the OpenAPI tag (healthz/readyz/version collapse under 'meta'), handler symbol becomes operationId, and ROUTE_TABLE leading // comments surface as operation.description. Request/response bodies are intentionally permissive in V1 — tighten incrementally. Wired via 'bun run gen:openapi' / 'bun run gen:openapi:check'; check:all appends gen:openapi:check after gen:docs:check. js-yaml (already a runtime dep) handles emission.

Golden snapshot pattern (warren-8aa4 / pl-7b06 step 22): src/server/__golden__/responses/ holds one JSON file per stable HTTP envelope (renderError + notFound/methodNotAllowed/notImplemented variants); companion test src/server/responses.golden.test.ts diffs live producer output against the fixtures. Regenerate intentionally with WARREN_UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 bun test src/server/responses.golden.test.ts and inspect git diff before committing — a noisy diff means the producer was destabilised. Directory name mirrors burrow's parser __golden__ convention and is already excluded from check:file-sizes, check:debt-markers, check:duplicates, and Biome's filename-convention rule. New golden dirs MUST keep the __golden__ name so existing exclusions in scripts/check-file-sizes.ts, scripts/check-debt-markers.ts, .jscpd.json, and biome.json keep applying. AGENTS.md 'Golden snapshots' section documents the pattern; validate-agents-md.ts's backtick-path heuristic flags tokens with '/' so prose mentions of '__golden__' without slashes are safe.

'bun run test:pg' (warren-0d10 / pl-da5b step 1) wraps scripts/test-pg.ts: boots postgres:16 as the warren-test-pg container on host port 55432 (override --port=N), waits for pg_isready, then runs 'bun test' with WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=postgres + WARREN_TEST_PG_URL pointed at it so isPostgresTestEnabled() branches (src/db/testing.ts) light up. Mirrors ci-postgres.yml env contract exactly. Flags: --keep (leave container running for psql poking), --reuse (attach to existing warren-test-pg instead of failing). Args after '--' forwarded verbatim to bun test; positional args forwarded too. Requires docker daemon — script exits with a copy-paste hint if missing.

scripts/hooks/pre-commit (warren-8544 / pl-da5b step 5) collapsed to 'exec bun run check:all' — single source of truth so the local pre-commit gate cannot diverge from CI (the original divergence kicked off warren-008a/pl-da5b). Hook discovery relies on the package.json 'prepare' script setting core.hooksPath=scripts/hooks; the guard was tightened from '[ -d .git ]' to '[ -e .git ]' so the hook installs in git worktrees / burrow sandboxes where .git is a file pointer, not a directory. The check:ci-parity guard (warren-6296) keeps the bun-run scripts reachable from check:all, so adding/removing a CI step automatically flows into the hook.

warren-ace1 / pl-da5b step 7 validation: bun run check:all is fully green end-to-end provided node_modules is populated (bun install first). Stale node_modules symptom: scripts/check-ci-parity.test.ts cannot resolve js-yaml at test time, contributes 0 covered functions, All-files functions coverage drops from ~86% to 83.22% (below 85% floor), check:coverage exits with a misleading 'add tests' message. Class B regression (missing-artifact reference in AGENTS.md) is reliably caught by 'bun run validate:agents-md' inside check:all: appending a backticked nonexistent path produces a [missing-path] failure and non-zero exit. Class A regression (postgres FK schema-qualified as public.projects) is mirrored locally by 'bun run test:pg' (NOT in check:all because it boots a docker container); when docker is absent the script exits loudly with an install-docker hint rather than skipping. Operator runbook: before tagging a release that touches src/db/migrations/postgres/, run 'bun run test:pg' on a docker-capable host; CI ci-postgres.yml is the merge gate.

Release patch flow (warren-325d / pl-5516 step 3) per .claude/commands/release.md: edit package.json version + src/index.ts VERSION in lockstep (release.yml fails if they disagree), move [Unreleased] entries to a new dated section in CHANGELOG.md, then run 'bun run gen:openapi' — docs/openapi.yaml's info.version is sourced from package.json, so gen:openapi:check fails check:all on drift. No CLAUDE.md/README.md edits needed for a pure patch with no command-surface change. Also: a fresh workspace needs 'bun install' before 'bun run check:all' — otherwise module-resolving tests fail (js-yaml, @os-eco/burrow-cli, @os-eco/plot-cli, drizzle-orm/bun-sqlite) and drag function coverage to ~83%, below the 85% floor.

Handlers monolith split phase 1 (warren-48de / pl-9088 step 1): src/server/handlers.ts → src/server/handlers/index.ts; plot handlers extracted to src/server/handlers/plots.ts (15 handlers covering /plots/*, /brainstorm), plot-plan-run handler to src/server/handlers/plot-plan-runs.ts. Pattern for the remaining 11 plan-9088 steps: (1) keep ROUTE_TABLE in handlers/index.ts as the canonical wire surface — gen:docs (scripts/generate-docs.ts HANDLERS_PATH) and gen:openapi (scripts/generate-openapi.ts) parse the array literal from one file. (2) Shared parsing helpers (readJsonBody, readJsonBodyOrEmpty, requireString, optionalString, requireParam, defaultSpawn) are exported from handlers/index.ts and re-imported by extracted modules — circular import is safe because handler bodies reference helpers lazily at request time, not at module evaluation. (3) Relative imports inside handlers/index.ts shift ../X → ../../X and ./X → ../X (siblings under src/server moved up one level). (4) ROUTE_TABLE imports the extracted handler functions from ./plots.ts and ./plot-plan-runs.ts. (5) Touch points: biome.json complexity-rule overrides, scripts/file-size-budgets.json, src/server/server.ts + src/server/index.ts + src/server/handlers.preview.test.ts + src/preview/proxy.test.ts all reference ./handlers/index.ts. (6) AGENTS.md grep-validates backticked paths so the handlers.ts → handlers/index.ts reference must be updated there too. (7) biome formatter may reformat single-line imports into multi-line when ../../ paths push them past 100 chars — budget for a small line-count uptick after biome --write and trim doc-comments to compensate so the file-size ratchet doesn't fail. (8) Plot-related test files moved alongside (handlers.plots.test.ts → handlers/plots.test.ts) with bulk sed-update of ../X → ../../X and ./X → ../X; inline dynamic imports (await import('../plots/index.ts')) need the same shift.

Bundle-size ratchet (warren-5abc) has a documented escape hatch for foundational growth: when a Phase legitimately expands the surface (e.g. warren-23fe Phase 1 design tokens added the status palette, radius/shadow/z scales, brand color, and self-hosted font CSS), raise the floors in scripts/bundle-size-budgets.json with an inline $comment_<bucket>_<date> field naming the seed + plan step + what was added + what was trimmed to minimise the bump + the named child seeds that must hold flat against the new floor. This mirrors the file-size grandfather pattern: a one-time legitimate raise with a tracker rationale, not a silent ratchet weakening. Don't use this for casual additions or dep bumps — those still owe a code-split or trim. For warren-23fe CSS went raw 34474->35200 (+726B) / gzip 6960->7160 (+200B); we trimmed unused -foreground pairs (success/warning/info/danger/neutral/card/accent), --shadow-xl, and --z-base to keep the bump minimal.

src/server/bridge-reconnect.ts (extracted from bridges.ts to stay under the file-size ratchet) holds runWithReconnect: after BRIDGE_STALL_THRESHOLD=3 consecutive errored reconnects with no forward progress it emits a one-shot bridge_stalled (stream:system) event, and bridge_recovered once events stream again (result.written>0 resets the counter + clears the stall). UI: RunDetail.tsx isBridgeStalled() scans the events tail and renders a warning Alert banner while !isTerminal. Shared bridge test fixtures now live in src/server/bridges.test-helpers.ts. Gotcha: the raw-JS bundle budget (584000) sits at its temporary pl-55a3 ceiling with ~0 headroom, so the banner only fit after trimming dev tracker/SPEC refs from user-facing title= tooltips.
